title: Add a method for setting a comment in to-be-created history items
body:
This patch adds `HistoryComment()` and `SetHistoryComment()` methods to the builder structure, which provide a way to manipulate which value we'll use when creating history items for images when we need them.

It stops providing a default for the `buildah config --created-by` option, so that if it's invoked a second time after setting the option, we won't override the previously-supplied value with our default.

It adds tests for --history-comment, --shell, --domainname, --hostname, and --comment.

comments.body: Are these History Fields that things that are used for caching?

:pushpin: Commit 7a14828 has been approved by `rhatdan`

<!-- @rh-atomic-bot r=rhatdan 7a14828068b71460441430c11f7d86fe7384b680 -->
:zap: Test exempted: pull fully rebased and already tested.

@TomSweeneyRedHat The history fields, if they're set (some, like `Comment`, are optional), should be showing up in `inspect` output even before these changes.
@rhatdan I wouldn't expect them to be used for caching.
